Title: Novel plant cell suspension platforms for saffron apocarotenoid production and its impact on carotenoid and volatile profiles

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Water-soluble saffron pigments with pharmaceutical and cosmetic value
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Transgenic Nicotiana cell suspensions and leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Present in constructs; in many N. benthamiana transformants absent due to partial T-DNA integration; included in successful CCD4a #7 line and BY-2 lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves crocin stability/accumulation when co-expressed with CCD genes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable co-expression in transgenic lines; genotyping; LC-MS crocin profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory engineering gene for stabilizing/boosting crocin production when stacked with CCDs and precursor-enhancing genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsUGT91P3 was co-engineered with CCDs and PaCrtB to support crocin production. 2. The study states that CsUGT91P1/UGT91P3 improves crocin stability. 3. Loss of this transgene in some N. benthamiana transformants accompanied poor crocin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The Summary mentions CsUGT93P1, but the Results/Methods and constructs consistently describe CsUGT91P3; extraction follows the main body text.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated after methyl jasmonate elicitation; significant changes at 30 min and later time points
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after methyl jasmonate elicitation in BY-2 CCD4a R2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with methyl jasmonate-induced increase in crocin accumulation by boosting carotenoid precursor pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR during elicitation time course; crocin quantification
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive endogenous target for boosting carotenoid flux in engineered BY-2 or tobacco systems
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtPSY1 was induced by methyl jasmonate in BY-2 CCD4a R2 cells. 2. Its induction correlated with increased crocin accumulation after elicitation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary engineered focal gene; included because directly analyzed in Results in relation to elicitor-driven metabolite increase.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Overexpressed after methyl jasmonate elicitation; significant differences at 24 h and later
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed after methyl jasmonate elicitation in BY-2 CCD4a R2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with methyl jasmonate-induced increase in crocin accumulation by supporting beta-carotene branch flux
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR during elicitation time course; crocin quantification
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential endogenous engineering target to support precursor supply for GjCCD4a-mediated apocarotenoid production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtLCYb expression increased after methyl jasmonate treatment in BY-2 CCD4a R2 cells. 2. This response accompanied elevated crocin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the central engineered gene of the paper.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in wild-type BY-2 context per discussion; downregulated after methyl jasmonate elicitation in CCD4a R2
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in elicited BY-2 CCD4a R2 samples
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression changes indicate altered carotenoid hydroxylation context during elicitor response and carotenoid remodeling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathway-balancing target when optimizing precursor partitioning toward saffron apocarotenoids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtCrtR-1 was downregulated after methyl jasmonate elicitation in BY-2 CCD4a R2. 2. The paper links high CrtR-1 expression with violaxanthin/neoxanthin accumulation in wild-type BY-2.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive endogenous gene, not a primary core engineering gene.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Saffron apocarotenoid biosynthesis; carotenoid cleavage pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Zeaxanthin/lutein cleavage branch leading to crocetin dialdehyde-derived saffron apocarotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed cleavage step / branch entry to saffron apocarotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the first and most critical step in saffron apocarotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Crocetin dialdehyde and HTCC-related cleavage products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cleaves carotenoids to generate saffron apocarotenoid precursors, including crocetin dialdehyde and HTCC-related products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical and cosmetic value; saffron quality-related metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Transgenic Nicotiana cell suspensions and leaves
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursors of target apocarotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher CsCCD2 expression in BY-2 high-crocin lines; in N. benthamiana T2 expression decreased versus T0; selected CCD2 #3 line productive under light
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes crocin accumulation in transgenic N. benthamiana and BY-2 cells, but generally less effectively than GjCCD4a in BY-2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in N. benthamiana and BY-2; RT-qPCR; LC-MS metabolite profiling; carotenoid HPLC-PDA profiling; bioreactor cultivation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light versus dark affected crocin output in N. benthamiana; methyl jasmonate did not increase crocins in N. benthamiana CCD2 #3
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous enzyme for engineering saffron apocarotenoids, but in tobacco BY-2 was inferior to GjCCD4a; may require stacking with precursor-boosting genes and platform-specific optimization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Carotenoid pathway remodeling; reduced violaxanthin in high-expression lines; transgene silencing/deletion in stable N. benthamiana plants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling (LC-MS, HPLC-PDA, GC-MS for related platform effects)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCCD2 enabled crocin production in stable N. benthamiana and BY-2 systems. 2. Higher CsCCD2 expression correlated with higher crocin accumulation in BY-2 lines. 3. In BY-2, CsCCD2 was much less productive than GjCCD4a. 4. In N. benthamiana, productive line CCD2 #3 performed best under light and supported bioreactor production. 5. CsCCD2 expression was associated with carotenoid remodeling including phytoene increase and lowered violaxanthin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses that CsCCD2 likely uses zeaxanthin and/or lutein depending on platform; beta-carotene is not considered its preferred substrate here.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Saffron apocarotenoid biosynthesis; carotenoid cleavage pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Beta-carotene/zeaxanthin cleavage branch leading to crocins, picrocrocin, HTCC, and volatile apocarotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed cleavage step / branch entry to saffron apocarotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the first and most critical step in saffron apocarotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Crocetin dialdehyde and HTCC-related cleavage products; volatile apocarotenoid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cleaves carotenoids, especially beta-carotene/zeaxanthin context in BY-2, producing saffron apocarotenoid precursors and volatile cleavage products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical/cosmetic value; aroma compound production; beta-cyclocitral has agronomic signaling value
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Transgenic Nicotiana cell suspensions and leaves
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursors of target apocarotenoids and volatile cleavage products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher GjCCD4a expression in BY-2 red lines than orange lines; expression decreased from T0 to T2 in N. benthamiana CCD4a #7 but remained productive
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes crocins, HTCC, picrocrocin and volatile apocarotenoids; highest-performing gene in BY-2 where red lines accumulated up to ~1.1 mg/g DW crocins and massive beta-cyclocitral
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in N. benthamiana and BY-2; RT-qPCR; LC-MS quantification; HPLC-PDA carotenoids; GC-MS volatiles; methyl jasmonate elicitation; wave bioreactor cultivation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark favored crocin accumulation in N. benthamiana CCD4a #7; methyl jasmonate increased crocin accumulation 1.4-fold in BY-2 CCD4a R2 after 72 h; bioreactor conditions altered product profile
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for heterologous production of saffron apocarotenoids and beta-cyclocitral in plant cell cultures; should be paired with precursor enhancement and process optimization for scale-up
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong carotenoid depletion/remodeling in high-expression BY-2 red lines, especially low beta-carotene and violaxanthin; however no growth penalty observed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GjCCD4a outperformed CsCCD2 in BY-2, producing far higher crocins, HTCC and picrocrocin. 2. High GjCCD4a expression defined red BY-2 lines with strongest apocarotenoid output. 3. GjCCD4a red lines produced extremely high beta-cyclocitral and detectable safranal. 4. GjCCD4a caused dramatic carotenoid remodeling, with lycopene and phytoene accumulation and depletion of beta-carotene/violaxanthin. 5. BY-2 CCD4a R2 supported elicitor-responsive and scalable production in bioreactors.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer beta-carotene is a preferred substrate for GjCCD4a in BY-2 because beta-carotene remained low while beta-cyclocitral dominated the volatile profile.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream / committed entry into carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Discussed as bottleneck for high carotenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl diphosphate to form phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supplies precursor pool for carotenoids and derived saffron apocarotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Transgenic Nicotiana cell suspensions and leaves
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target apocarotenoid production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low in N. benthamiana stable lines; similar to CCD expression in BY-2 lines; both low and high expression associated with carotenoid accumulation in BY-2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts carotenoid pathway and is associated with phytoene, beta-carotene and xanthophyll accumulation, supporting downstream saffron apocarotenoid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable co-expression in transgenic N. benthamiana and BY-2; RT-qPCR; carotenoid HPLC-PDA; apocarotenoid LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Platform-dependent expression; stable constitutive expression in N. benthamiana often low or lost after T-DNA integration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important upstream engineering module to raise carotenoid flux when stacked with CCD genes; likely essential for maximizing productivity in heterologous platforms
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May cause strong carotenoid accumulation and pathway remodeling; stable integration/expression problems in N. benthamiana under constitutive promoter
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaCrtB was introduced to enhance carotenoid biosynthesis upstream of apocarotenoid formation. 2. In BY-2, lines with low CCD expression but PaCrtB expression accumulated beta-carotene and violaxanthin, showing CrtB-driven carotenoid enhancement. 3. The study discusses phytoene synthase as a bottleneck for high carotenoid accumulation. 4. In stable N. benthamiana, PaCrtB was frequently absent or weakly expressed, limiting benefit.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although co-engineered rather than the sole focus, PaCrtB is a core experimental gene directly manipulated to affect nutrient-metabolite output.
